data.title: Indian businesses delivered higher shareholder returns in 10 of 14 major sectors: DSP
data.description:
The report adds that while FII outflows have been in focus, net FDI into India in the September quarter also fell. Outflows during the period were around $2.75 billion, making it the fourth quarterly outflow since data became available for India in 2000.

Indian entrepreneurs are better skilled to navigate challenges and 10 of 14 major sectors delivered higher shareholder returns, says DSP Mutual Fund.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Indian businesses have delivered higher shareholder returns in ten of the fourteen major sectors over the past 20 years, according to the latest newsletter by DSP. These sectors include aerospace & defence, banks, automobile, FMCG and insurance among others. The only exceptions, according to the report are oil, telecom and power.

For example, in the aerospace & defence space, India leader Bharat Electronics has a 20-year average RoE of 21.2 while the global leader RTX Corp has a 20-year average ROE of 16.4. In terms of 20-year EPS CAGR it is 14 percent and 1 percent, respectively. Similarly, in the case of banks, HDFC Banks RoE is 17.9 against JP Morgan Chase and Co's 11.1.  In terms of EPS CAGR, HDFC stands at 21 percent and JP Morgan Chase at 12 percent.

The report highlights the fact that based on earnings, Indian businesses have showcased one of the best earnings per share growth over the last 20 years.

“Economic growth, political stability, funds flows, demographics and (insert any other narrative) is easily falsifiable. Meaning, despite these triggers, many other countries do not have businesses which have delivered these numbers consistently,” stated the report while adding that the reason could be the ability of Indian entrepreneur to navigate challenges across cycles and yet deliver superior results. Another reason could be the focus on minimising inputs costs by Indian entrepreneurs.

What else is driving the market?

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Meanwhile, the report adds that another key factor contributing to the outperformance of Indian markets is the consistent growth in the book value of underlying companies. “Over the long term, more than three fourths of companies have shown positive growth in their book value. Notably, in the 20 years, 7 out of 39 companies with positive growth in book value have achieved growth in each of those 20 years, even amidst global challenges such as the GFC and the COVID-19 pandemic, highlighting the stability of these businesses,” said the report adding that a similar case is seen in the broader Indian market (Nifty 500).

FII, FDI outflow concerns remain

The report adds that while FII outflows have been in focus, net FDI into India in the September quarter also fell. Outflows during the period were around $2.75 billion, making it the fourth quarterly outflow since data became available for India in 2000.

The report notes that despite the positive outlook on India, foreign investors are selling both, in the secondary equity market and the typically more stable direct investment route. “One possible driver is the high valuations offered by Indian markets, which contrasts with the low valuation multiples available anywhere else in the world,” stated the report.

Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by investment experts on Moneycontrol.com are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol.com advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.

data.title: Zen Tech hits record high as Nuvama expresses optimism over MoU with AVT Simulation
data.description:
Zen Technologies Ltd

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

Zen Technologies shares surged nearly 8 percent to a lifetime high of Rs 2,033 after Nuvama Institutional Equities reiterated its 'Buy' rating on the stock, setting a target price of Rs 2,200. This implies an upside of 16.5 percent from the stock's previous close.

The brokerage's bullish stance followed Zen Tech signing a memorandum of understanding (MoU) with Florida-based AVT Simulation to penetrate the US defense market. The collaboration was announced during the National Training & Simulation Association's Interservice/Industry Training, Simulation, and Education Conference.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

"This is strategically positive for Zen Tech, which shall expand its serviceable market (details awaited) for both simulation training and anti-drone segments while offering (Unmanned Aerial System) UAS-technology to AVT," Nuvama said.

Follow our live blog for all the market action

At 10:30 AM, shares of Zen Tech were trading 3 percent higher at Rs 1,943. The stock has delivered a staggering 147 percent gain year-to-date, far outpacing the Nifty 50's 14 percent rise during the same period. Notably, Zen Tech has been posting positive monthly returns for four months now.

Nuvama expects Zen Tech to achieve a 50 percent revenue CAGR, 35 percent operating profit margins, and 25 percent PAT margins over the next two to three years. The brokerage said that a pickup in order inflows in H2 FY25 is crucial to sustaining this trajectory.

"The US is currently one of the biggest markets for defence exports from India. However, we await further details on this partnership between Zen Tech and AVT Simulation to gauge the opportunity size," Nuvama added.

Also Read | Zen Tech shares soar on strategic pact with Florida-based simulation company

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Zen Tech's Chairman and Managing Director Ashok Atluri said, "Through this partnership, AVT Simulation shall help Zen Tech break into the US defence market, and Zen Tech shall help AVT expand its international business."

Robert Abascal, Founder and Chief Strategic Officer of AVT Simulation, highlighted the importance of counter-UAS technology, calling it a 'big cost driver' in light of lessons learned from modern conflicts, including the war in Ukraine.

In Q2FY25, Zen Tech's profit surged 309 percent to Rs 62.6 crore, up from Rs 15.3 crore last year. Revenue grew 263 percent from Rs 66.5 crore to Rs 241.8 crore.

data.title: Zaggle Prepaid Services surges 4% after three order wins in two days
data.description:
Zaggle Prepaid shares have gained over 24 percent in the past month.

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

Shares of Zaggle Prepaid Ocean Services rose over 4 percent on December 6 after the company secured three orders in just two days. With the gains in today's session, the stock also hit an intraday hit of Rs 521, sitting just an inch away from its record high of Rs 523.30.

At 10.38 am, Zaggle Prepaid shares were trading at Rs 519.40 on the NSE, reflecting a gain of over 24 percent in the past month.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The first order won by the company is a five year contract for Zaggle's fleet program from AGP City Gas. Following that, the company won an one year order from Hitachi India. Under this order, Zaggle would provide Hitachi India with its Zaggle propel rewards solution.

Aside from these, the company also signed a two year master agreement with Blink Commerce to provide its Zoyer solution.

Follow our live blog for all the market action

Apart from these orders, the company is also expected to see an inflow of new orders after its seven-year contract that Zaggle Prepaid signed with MasterCard Technology recently.

In November, Zaggle inked a seven-year agreement with Mastercard for product distribution. As part of the deal, Mastercard will recommend Zaggle's SaaS platform, payment solutions, and card products to corporate clients and other ecosystem entities on a referral basis. In an exclusive interaction with NDTV Profit about the partnership, Avinash Godkhindi, Zaggle’s Managing Director and CEO, stated the agreement as a "great win" for the company.

"Mastercard conducted an extensive evaluation of several spend management companies before selecting Zaggle to offer this solution within their ecosystem. This partnership holds significant value for us in both the short and medium to long term," Godkhindi explained, highlighting that the integration with Mastercard is already complete.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

He further added that contracts under the partnership are expected to materialise soon, potentially as early as the next quarter.

The West Bengal-based company offers construction and allied services for industrial civil projects, residential & commercial buildings, road, railway infrastructure, power and water distribution projects, specialising in engineering, procurement, and construction (EPC) services. It has an order book of Rs 574.9 crore as of August 31, comprising 41 ongoing projects.

Ganesh Infraworld intends to spend Rs 70 crore out of the net issue proceeds for its working capital requirements; and the remainder for general corporate purposes. It will use Rs 40 crore in current financial year and Rs 30 crore in next fiscal 2026 for its working capital.

data.title: Rate sensitive stocks mixed: bank, auto stocks rise after RBI slashes CRR by 50 bps; realty stocks sulk
data.description:
RBI revised full year FY25 GDP growth to 6.6 percent from 7.2 percent

Rate-sensitive sectors like banks, autos, and real estate showed a mixed performance on December 6, following the Reserve Bank of India's decision to reduce the cash reserve ratio (CRR) by 50 basis points to 4.5 percent, easing growth-related investor concerns. However, the RBI maintained the repo rate at 6.5 percent, aligning with market expectations, and reaffirmed its "neutral" policy stance.

Nifty Bank and Auto indices jumped 0.3 percent each following the RBI MPC decision, while Nifty Realty index was down by 0.6 percent.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The Monetary Policy Committee decided by 4-2 majority to keep interest rates unchanged at 6.5 percent, ever since February 2023. On the other hand, the SDF rate remained steady at 6.5 percent and MSF rate was at 6.75 percent.

Governor Shaktikanta Das, in his address, stated that food inflation is expected to remain elevated during Q3 and some easing is likely from Q4FY25. "High inflation impacts consumers by reducing their disposable income, which in turn affects spending and overall economic dynamics," he added.

On the GDP growth front, the RBI revised full year FY25 growth to 6.6 percent from 7.2 percent. This comes on the back of disappointing second quarter GDP growth that fell to 7-quarter low of 5.4 percent YoY. Meanwhile, Q3FY25 GDP growth was downgraded to 6.8 percent from 7.4 percent, while Q4FY25 growth forecast was cut to 7.2 percent from 7.4 percent.

For the next fiscal year (FY26), Q1 GDP growth was also cut to 6.9 percent from 7.3 percent. Q2FY26 GDP growth has been pegged at 7.3 percent.

Meanwhile, inflation projections have largely been revised to the higher end. For full fiscal year FY25, inflation was upgraded to 4.8 percent from 4.5 percent.

data.description:
Jefferies retained its 'buy' call on Newgen Software with a price target of Rs 1,500.

  • Watchlist

  • Set Alert
 
 
live

  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

Shares of Newgen Software rose 3.5 percent after international brokerage Jefferies states that it remains constructive on the company's growth prospects, anticipating a 15 percent upside from Thursday's closing price.

The brokerage retained its 'buy' call on the stock with a price target of Rs 1,500.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

At 10.17 am, shares of Newgen Software were trading at Rs 1,338.15 on the NSE. With this, the stock is up over 13 percent for the past week.

Jefferies remains confident on the management's strong growth outlook, largely driven by its India and West Asia businesses. The management continues to step up efforts in the US, especially among mid-large tier banks, Jefferies noted.

Follow our live blog for all the market action

Last week, Newgen Software bagged a Rs 32.5 crore order from the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) to implement and maintain the Regulatory Application Management System (RAMS), slated to be implemented over a period of seven years.

Jefferies believes that this recent purchase order from RBI may further drive up deal from other Indian banks for Newgen. On that account, Jefferies feels continuous order inflows and new client additions will support growth for the company.

In the September quarter, Newgen Software reported a 47 percent on-quarter growth in its consolidated net profit to Rs 70.3 crore, while revenue from operations grew 14.8 percent to Rs 361.2 crore. On a year-on-year basis, net profit also surged 47 percent, while revenue rose 23 percent.

data.title: MPC meet: RBI cuts India's GDP growth projection to 6.6%
data.description:
India's GDP growth for Q2 FY25 slowed to 5.4%, marking the lowest growth in two years, down from 8.1% in the same period last year.

The Reserve Bank of India on December 6 cut its GDP growth forecast to 6.6 percent for current fiscal year, from 7.2 percent earlier.

The central bank also revised its estimate for Q3 to 7.4 percent from 6.8 percent earlier and Q4 to 7.4 percent from 7.2 percent.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

For Q1 FY26, the central bank revised its GDP projection to 6.9 percent from the earlier 7.3 percent and to 7.3 percent for the September quarter of FY26.

India's economic growth slowed down in the September quarter of FY25 to a 7-quarter low of 5.4 percent on contraction in mining growth, manufacturing and utility services. The second quarter number has now clouded the outlook for the full year growth estimates, according to experts.

However, cutting rates to boost growth won't be as easy an option. Annual retail inflation quickened to 6.21% in October, breaching the central bank's tolerance band for the first time in more than a year.

In the June quarter, GDP growth fell to 5-quarter low of 6.7 percent.

MPC decided to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged at 6.5 percent on December 6.
data.author: MoneyControl

Suraksha Diagnostic shares listed at a discount against the issue price of Rs 441 per share on December 6.

Integrated diagnostic chain Suraksha Diagnostic Ltd stock started the trade at Rs 438 per share on the NSE, a discount of 0.68 percent from the issue price on the NSE.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The company's market valuation stood at Rs 2,281.14 crore.

Suraksha Diagnostic IPO allottees may see modest listing gains on Dec 6; analysts recommend trading strategy

The initial public offer of the company got fully subscribed on the last day of share sale on December 3. The Rs 846-crore initial share sale received bids for 1.70 crore shares against 1.34 crore shares on offer, translating into 1.27 times subscription.

Earlier, the company mobilised Rs 254 crore from anchor investors. The Initial Public Offering (IPO) has a price range of Rs 420-441 apiece.

The Kolkata-headquartered company's IPO consists of an offer for sale (OFS) of 19,189,330 equity shares worth Rs 846.25 crore, at the upper end of the price band, by promoters and investor shareholders, with no fresh issue component.

Since the issue is completely an OFS, the company will not receive any proceeds and all the funds will go to the selling shareholders.

data.title: Angel One shares drop 5% after client acquisition falls for second straight month
data.description:
Angel One shares rose over 30 percent in the last three months.

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

Shares of Angel One fell close to 5 percent on December 6 after the company reported a decline in gross client acquisitions during November, the second month in a row.

Its gross client acquisition fell to 60 lakh in November, down from 70 lakh last month. On a month-on-month basis, the gross client acquisition fell 14.6 percent, while it was down nearly 12 percent on a year-on-year basis.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

At 09.46 am, shares of Angel One were trading at Rs 3,106.95 on the NSE.

Follow our market blog to catch all the live action

Apart from that, total platform orders also fell 23.5 percent month-on-month to 13.09 crore. Average daily orders also dipped 6.5 percent on-month to 72.8 lakh from October’s 77.8 lakh, but rose 35.7 percent on a yearly basis, the company stated in its exchange filing.

It's worth noting that November had only 18 trading days compared to 22 in October and 20 in November last year which would've also influenced Angel One's performance during the month.

The Overall Average Daily Turnover (ADTO) declined by 12 percent month-on-month, with the Futures & Options segment witnessing a 12.5 percent drop.

Angel One's overall client base reached 2.8 crore by November-end, up 2 percent month-on-month and 56 percent year-on-year.

data.title: Angel One shares fall 4% over tepid November business performance
data.description:
Angel One shares fall 4% over tepid November business performance

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

Shares of Angel One fell 4% on December 6 as the brokerage firm posted a muted business performance in November on sequential basis.

Angel One said, in a regulatory filing, that its gross client acquisition fell to 0.6 million from 0.7 million last month. On a month-on-month basis, the gross client acquisition fell 14.6%, while the figure was lower by 11.9% on a year-on-year basis.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The number of orders on the platform also fell by 23.5% on a sequential basis to 130.96 million. However, on a year-on-year basis, the figure increased by 22.2%.

At 9:25 am on December 6, Angel One shares were trading 4% lower at Rs 3,091 apiece. The market capitalisation of the stock is Rs Rs 27,800 crore. Its 52-week high is Rs 3,896 and 52-week low is Rs 2,025.

Overall Average Daily Turnover (ADTO) fell 12% on an overall basis when compared to November, while for the Futures & Options segment, the ADTO was down by 12.5% from last month. The ADTO for both these metrics increased by 24.1% and 23.3% year-on-year, respectively.
